title: Number of people employed in fisheries and aquaculture
caption:
Number of people employed in fisheries and aquaculture

This line chart tracks the global number of people employed in fisheries and aquaculture from 1995 to 2018. The vertical axis measures employment in millions, ranging from 0 to 40 million, while the horizontal axis marks years at five-year intervals plus 2018. Two distinct lines represent the data: an orange line for "Fishers" and a blue line for "Fish farmers (aquaculture)." The Fishers line begins just below 30 million in 1995, rises steadily to approximately 35 million by 2000, and continues climbing to a peak near 40 million in 2010. From 2010 to 2018, the line remains relatively flat, hovering just under the 40 million mark. The Fish farmers line starts at roughly 8 million in 1995, increases to about 13 million by 2000, reaches 15 million in 2005, and climbs to nearly 19 million by 2010. Between 2010 and 2018, this line shows minimal growth, ending slightly above 20 million. Throughout the entire period, the number of Fishers remains substantially higher than the number of Fish farmers, with the gap between the two lines widening until around 2010 and then stabilizing. Both categories show consistent growth over the first 15 years, followed by a plateau in the final eight years of the chart.
